_comment: Data-retention / logging / ZDR posture per hosted gateway and first-party model cloud, from primary sources (official docs, privacy policies, DPAs). Answers 'who sees/logs/trains on my prompts through this gateway?'. Self-hosted gateways are omitted: you own the data plane, so retention is whatever you configure. 'not documented' = the official docs are silent, NOT 'no'. On every router, real exposure is set by the UPSTREAM you route to, not just the router's own policy — enable a ZDR-only / no-training-providers filter, don't just trust the router. Verify against live policy before an audit.

legend.logs_bodies_by_default: does it store prompt+completion bodies by default? yes | no | configurable | not documented
legend.zdr: zero-data-retention / no-log mode: default | opt-in (self-serve) | approval-gated | none | not documented
legend.trains_on_prompts: does the operator train on your prompts? no | opt-out | upstream-depends | yes-by-tos | not documented (routers: 'own' policy vs 'upstream')

gateways.name: Cloudflare AI Gateway
gateways.zdr: opt-in (self-serve opt-out; no formal ZDR mode)
gateways.retention: storage-limit based (Free 100k logs/acct, Paid 10M/gateway), no time period
gateways.caveat: Bodies persist by default — you must opt out (`cf-aig-collect-log-payload: false`); no documented ZDR guarantee or training statement.

gateways.name: Portkey (cloud)
gateways.caveat: Self-serve toggle suppresses log payloads; the strongest 'nothing stored' guarantee is enablement-on-request.

gateways.name: Vercel AI Gateway
gateways.zdr: default (own layer); enforce upstream-ZDR via toggle/per-request
gateways.caveat: 'ZDR by default' = Vercel's own handling only; requests can still hit non-ZDR upstreams unless you enable the filters. Team-wide ZDR + training controls launched 2025-26.

gateways.name: Helicone (cloud)
gateways.caveat: It's a logging proxy — stores bodies by default. Retention numbers live only on the pricing page; binding legal docs are 2023-24 vintage and silent on LLM data/training.

gateways.name: Requesty
gateways.retention: 0d default; 30d encrypted (EU) if logging enabled; upstreams vary
gateways.caveat: Router defaults to no body storage, but enabling logging or routing to a retaining upstream means prompts persist downstream.

gateways.name: OpenRouter
gateways.trains_on_prompts: no (own); upstream-depends — some free-tier routes train
gateways.retention: 0d unless prompt-logging opted in; upstream shown per-endpoint
gateways.caveat: Opt-outs govern WHICH upstreams get data, not what they do — verify the specific endpoint; free-tier routes can still train. Policy actively maintained (updated 2026-07-06).

gateways.name: Eden AI
gateways.retention: not retained after processing (only billing/security metadata)
gateways.caveat: ZDR covers Eden AI's own layer only; the chosen upstream's practices are separate and explicitly excluded from Eden AI's responsibility.

gateways.name: Martian
gateways.retention: not documented ('as long as you use our Services')
gateways.caveat: ⚠️ Public ToS defines 'Customer Materials' to include your Input Data and grants Martian a license to use it 'to train and improve Martian's models' — no documented opt-out or ZDR toggle; only relief is a negotiated DPA. Do not send sensitive prompts without one.

gateways.name: Azure OpenAI
gateways.zdr: approval-gated (Modified Abuse Monitoring; Limited Access form)
gateways.trains_on_prompts: no (not used to train foundation models; not shared with OpenAI)
gateways.retention: no fixed day-count on current pages (the previously-documented 30-day figure is gone)
gateways.caveat: ⚠️ CHANGED 2025-26: rebranded 'Foundry Models sold by Azure' (Oct 2025); the '30 days' figure is no longer stated — storage now conditional on abuse-flagging, ZDR via approval only.

gateways.name: AWS Bedrock
gateways.trains_on_prompts: no (providers can't access prompts/completions); except opt-in `provider_data_share` models
gateways.retention: 0d default; up to 30d if `provider_data_share`; your own logs persist until deleted
gateways.caveat: New 2025-26 retention-mode system; some newer models REQUIRE `provider_data_share` and are blocked under `none` — ZDR and model access can conflict per-model.

gateways.name: Google Vertex AI
gateways.logs_bodies_by_default: configurable (⚠️ standard non-invoiced accounts ARE logged for abuse monitoring)
gateways.zdr: opt-in / enterprise-gated (disable caching + invoiced billing; DPA amendment)
gateways.trains_on_prompts: no (not used to train foundation models without permission)
gateways.retention: abuse logs up to 90d; default I/O caching up to 24h; 0d under full ZDR config
gateways.caveat: The permissive default is for enterprise/invoiced accounts; standard GCP-ToS accounts WITHOUT invoiced billing get prompts logged (up to 90d) unless they file the opt-out form, and caching must be disabled separately.

gateways.name: OpenAI (direct API)
gateways.zdr: approval-gated (via sales; then `store` is always false)
gateways.trains_on_prompts: no (API data not used to train, since 2023-03-01, unless you opt in — differs from ChatGPT consumer)
gateways.retention: 30d default; 0d with approved ZDR — but see caveat
gateways.caveat: ⚠️ NYT v. OpenAI preservation order (May 2025, affirmed Jun 2025, narrowed Sep 2025) forces OpenAI to PRESERVE output-log data — including API content normally deleted at 30d — under legal hold. Not reflected in the standard data-usage doc; the '0/30-day' deletion promise is currently subject to a litigation override for affected data.
